headlines.text: Day 30: Houthis fire missiles at Israel as US-Iran war enters second month with no end in sight
Millions join third round of 'No Kings' protests across all 50 states and multiple continents
Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt, Saudi Arabia meet in Islamabad to push for Iran ceasefire
Iran parliament speaker warns country is bracing for a US ground invasion despite peace talks
Dow enters correction territory, S&P posts fifth straight losing week — worst streak since 2022
Gas prices hit $3.98 national average — up a full dollar in just one month thanks to Iran war
Tiger Woods arrested for DUI after rolling his Land Rover near Jupiter Island home, released on bail
TSA agents to receive first paychecks Monday after weeks unpaid amid DHS partial shutdown
Project Hail Mary starring Ryan Gosling opens with massive $80.5M weekend — best for Amazon MGM ever
No Kings protesters arrested in downtown LA after crowd tries to tear down fence at federal detention center

breaking.text: BREAKING: Iran parliament speaker says Iranian forces are preparing for US ground operation on Day 30 of war

stories.hed: Houthis Join War, Diplomats Scramble, Iran Braces for Ground Invasion
stories.dek: The US-Iran war hit its one-month mark Sunday with Yemen's Houthis now lobbing missiles at Israel, at least 3,461 people killed in Iran, and foreign ministers from four countries holding emergency talks in Islamabad. Iran's parliament speaker helpfully announced the country is preparing for a US ground assault — which is either a military strategy or a very aggressive way to kill the peace talks.

stories.hed: Iran Threatens US University Campuses in Gulf Region Unless Strikes on Iranian Universities Stop
stories.dek: Iran's Revolutionary Guard gave the US until March 30 to officially condemn strikes on Iranian universities or face retaliatory attacks on American campuses in Qatar and UAE, where Texas A&M, Northwestern, and NYU all have outposts. Apparently the next campus visit brochure will include a new section on geopolitical risk.

stories.hed: Millions Turn Out for Third 'No Kings' Protest — Arrests Made in LA After Crowd Targets Federal Detention Center
stories.dek: More than 3,200 marches took place across all 50 states and several continents on Saturday, with the flagship rally in St. Paul, Minnesota. Robert De Niro, Jane Fonda, and Bruce Springsteen marched in New York. The evening's vibes shifted in LA when a subset of protesters tried to tear down a fence outside a federal detention center and were greeted with tear gas.

stories.hed: TSA Workers to Finally Get Paid Monday After Weeks of Working Without Checks During DHS Shutdown
stories.dek: After weeks of epic airport lines and unpaid federal screeners, Trump signed an executive order to get TSA agents paid — they should see checks as early as Monday, March 30. The partial government shutdown, which has also kept DHS funding in limbo, has produced the longest TSA wait times in history and a nice bipartisan tradition of everyone pointing fingers at everyone else.

stories.hed: Tiger Woods Arrested Again for DUI — Breathalyzer Said 0.00, But He Still Rolled His Land Rover and Refused the Urine Test
stories.dek: Tiger Woods, 50, was arrested Friday in Jupiter Island after his Land Rover clipped a truck, rolled over, and left him crawling out the passenger door while appearing 'lethargic.' He blew triple zeroes on the breathalyzer but refused a urine test, suggesting medications may have been involved — the same script as his 2017 arrest. Trump called him a 'very close friend' and said 'some difficulty,' which is doing a lot of heavy lifting as far as statements go.

markets.context: Markets posted their fifth straight weekly loss — the longest losing streak since 2022 — as surging oil prices driven by the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz disruptions crushed equities and sent the Dow and Nasdaq into correction territory.

gas.note: Up a full dollar in one month — your wallet is doing its own version of the Iran war.

popCulture.hed: Project Hail Mary Opens to $80.5M — Ryan Gosling Just Proved Movie Stars Still Exist
popCulture.dek: Ryan Gosling's sci-fi adaptation of Andy Weir's beloved novel scored the biggest opening weekend for a non-franchise film since Oppenheimer, shattering records for Amazon MGM. With 83% of audiences saying they would 'definitely recommend' it, Gosling may have just single-handedly saved the theatrical experience — and given everyone a two-hour escape from checking oil prices.

genZ.definition: Don Toliver's 'Call Back' off his Octane album spawned a viral TikTok choreography trend created by @mai world that has taken over For You Pages in late March 2026. Creators across every skill level are jumping in, with the comments full of people tagging friends to learn the smooth, hypnotic routine together.
genZ.usage: When Iran says 'no direct talks' but then the foreign ministers meet in Islamabad anyway: this is the geopolitical 'Call Back' and nobody learned the steps.

tldr: Day 30 of the US-Iran war arrived with Houthis launching missiles, diplomats speed-dating in Islamabad, and oil prices up 50% since hostilities began — while back home, millions marched in the streets, TSA agents are about to get their first paycheck in weeks, and Tiger Woods once again reminded everyone that his personal life is also a contact sport.
